evidence_quotes: I received a text message from Bank of America asking if I attempted to make a purchase for {$170.00} Krogers with my credit card on XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX.
He stated my card had been charged with two transactions and that they were calling to verify that they were fraud since they were suspicious.
He then told me they were charges in Arizona, and he could see that I am a Maryland resident.
He asked if I knew a XXXX XXXX.
He asked if I was trying to wire her {$5500.00}.
He then proceeded to give me my name, address, phone number, last 4 of account number and last 4 of my social security number.
He walked me though step by step on my account and informed me that I was submitting a wire reversal.
He had me put all my information in as the receiver, so I thought this was legit.

One representative I spoke to told me they try to resolve these cases quickly and that I should have an answer within 2 weeks and since it took them the rest of the week to get me the hold time of 40 minutes.
He said for security purposes those take a little longer.
He even said that if the person scammed more than one person and the account was already frozen it is very likely I will get my money back and he has seen people get their money back before.
They told me I would get an email if they heard back and I would see the money in my account.

I called back a few days later again and was told the same thing but that sometimes this process takes some time because XXXX XXXX might have to request the money from another bank if my money was transferred from that account to an account outside of XXXX XXXX.
I called back a few days later and spoke to someone who said it could take up to 90 days and that XXXX XXXX could not respond back to Bank of America.

Coinbase has had announced and unannounced breaches of data maintained on, and security of, its platform; leading to a variety of harms suffered by Coinbase account/wallet holders : support scams, penetrations of Coinbases platform leading to SIM swaps, failure to block blacklisted destination addresses, and account takeovers.

Coinbase is responsible for securing its platform and all internal and external cryptocurrency payment rails, which serve as the infrastructure that facilitates the transfer of digital currencies between parties.
